reasoning: Merbromin (mercurochrome) is an organomercury compound containing a mercury atom bonded to a fluorescein-derived organic scaffold, along with bromine substituents. However, in ChemOnt 2.1 the most fitting available label for organomercury compounds under organometallics maps to organometalloid/organoarsenic area; more precisely it is an organotransition metal compound, but mercury compounds are classified under organometalloid or organometallic categories. Given the available labels, the best fit is under Organometallic compounds as an organotransition metal compound since mercury is a transition metal.
